steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explicitly states: ‘greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use.’ The question asks what point the text makes about the difference in park usage between the two cities. The answer must match this stated conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — researcher expectations: The passage says nothing about what researchers expected to find. ✗
  • Choice B — historical sources: Historical sources are never mentioned as a factor in the park use difference. ✗
  • Choice C — data inaccuracy: The passage does not suggest the data is flawed or inaccurate. ✗
  • Choice D — proximity alone does not explain the difference: Restates the passage's explicit conclusion word for word. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
